findings.diabetes_subtype: Type 2 — insulin resistance dominant with inflammatory amplification

primary_medications.name: Metformin
primary_medications.rationale: First-line for T2D — reduces hepatic glucose output, improves insulin sensitivity

primary_medications.name: Semaglutide
primary_medications.rationale: Addresses insulin resistance pathway, cardiovascular benefit, weight reduction

findings.monitoring_plan: HbA1c every 3 months, renal function quarterly, liver enzymes at baseline and 3 months
findings.medication_summary: Metformin + Semaglutide (primary) + Empagliflozin (renal protection). Triple therapy targeting insulin resistance, inflammatory pathways, and nephropathy risk.

summary: Recommended triple therapy: Metformin (first-line), Semaglutide (GLP-1 RA for insulin resistance + CV benefit), Empagliflozin (SGLT2i for nephroprotection). Based on T2D with dominant inflammation and fibrosis markers.
